item.title: The Generation That Grew Up With A.I. Hates It
item.description: Why graduates are booing artificial intelligence.

item.title: The End of Reality TV Politics
item.description: Reality TV politics is coming to an end because, Sarah Isgur argues, reality TV itself is dying. On “The Opinions,” she explains why she believes voters are now gravitating toward more optimistic, Ted Lasso-style candidates instead.

item.title: Demon Attacks. Teleportation. Astrology. The Supernatural Is Back.
item.description: One of the oldest and most durable features of human experience is re-emerging.

item.title: This Redistricting Chaos Must End
item.description: First on Democrats’ to-do list must be banning gerrymandering, restoring voting rights protections and reforming the Supreme Court.

item.title: MAGA Women Are Leading a #Me2.0 in Washington
item.description: These G.O.P. crusaders might be the perfect people to fight sexual misconduct on Capitol Hill.

item.title: We’ve Become Too Savage for ‘Lord of the Flies’
item.description: Savagery just ain’t what it used to be.

item.title: Stephen Colbert’s Late Show Cancellation and the Fall of CBS
item.description: In shutting down a 33-year late-night franchise, CBS is assenting to its own diminishment.

item.title: The Vanity and Inanity of Trump’s White House
item.description: If you’re not strutting, you’re not selling

item.title: We Need More ‘Rabid Partisans’ Like Shane Massey
item.description: Defeating a sclerotic rump of a party is no achievement. Instead, one-party rule enables corruption. It fosters stagnation.

item.title: Palestinian Accounts of Rape by Israelis
item.description: Readers respond to a column by Nicholas Kristof. Also: Imposing religion.

item.title: Don’t Run Away From Fear. ‘Agree’ With It.
item.description: What does it mean to “make friends” with fear? On “The Ezra Klein Show,” the beloved Buddhist teacher and author Pema Chödrön explores how to embrace uncertainty and loss, rather than avoiding them.

item.title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
item.description: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power

item.title: Firing Squads Expose the Brutality of the Death Penalty
item.description: America is returning to firing squad executions after failing to find a more humane way to carry out the death penalty.

item.title: What A.I. Kant Do
item.description: Could we English majors have a future after all?

item.title: How the Jerks I Grew Up With on Long Island Took Over the Country
item.description: I noticed that a lot of TV villains come from the town I grew up in. I wasn’t surprised.

item.title: Tech Workers Have Fears About A.I., Too. They Can Do Something About It.
item.description: Even the developers of A.I. want regulation. They’re starting to take action.

item.title: This Is Why I Find Pema Chödrön So Essential
item.description: The 89-year-old Buddhist nun teaches the power of agreeing with your anxiety, instead of trying to run from it.

item.title: This Is Why I Find Pema Chödrön So Essential
item.description: The 89-year-old Buddhist nun teaches the power of agreeing with your anxiety, instead of trying to run from it.

item.title: Nothing Beats Polarization Like Civics Education
item.description: The decline of civic education hit bottom about a decade ago and is at last on the rebound. This fact brings me hope.

item.title: Getting Jeff Bezos to the Opera
item.description: Box office is waning. Donors are fading. Opera needs new lifeblood. The answer lies with tech fortunes.

item.title: Here’s the Trade Deal That Trump and Xi Should Have Reached
item.description: For this to happen, however, both men would need personality transplants.

item.title: The Great Disruption: How A.I. Will Shape Our Future
item.description: Readers discuss ways in which artificial intelligence is reshaping our lives. Also: Frequent redistricting.

item.title: France Has a New Picture of the Holocaust
item.description: A new collection of propaganda photos shows the first roundup of Jews in Paris, in 1941.

item.title: What A.I. Did to My College Class
item.description: Lessons from the first graduating A.I. class.

item.title: Trump Is Wasting Our Time
item.description: We are in a moment when we need to build and think to the future.

item.title: The Real Victim of Trump’s War on Numbers Is You
item.description: Authoritarians go after data. The president has already started.

item.title: The Law They Hate Was a High Point of Our History
item.description: The Voting Rights Act has more democratic legitimacy than the court that is trying to destroy it.

item.title: China’s Peak Is Now
item.description: Does Xi Jinping see the world this way?

item.title: The Great Political Realignment of 2026
item.description: The country seems to be tiring of the Trump era. Now what?

item.title: Why Does Health Care Cost So Much?
item.description: Readers, including health professionals, offer their analyses.

item.title: The Great Political Realignment of 2026
item.description: The country seems to be tiring of the Trump era. Now what?

item.title: This Is the Antidote to a Lonely Life of Screens
item.description: We crave connection. We’re inventing ways to find it.

item.title: Keir Starmer and the End of Britain’s Old Certainties
item.description: The decade since Brexit has been one long, painful process of trying — and failing — to make sense of this new reality.

item.title: The Revolt Against the Girl Bosses Has Finally Come
item.description: Some of this country’s most prominent girl bosses sound like they don’t even know how to read the room.

item.title: Why We Keep Tricking Ourselves Into Thinking A.I. Is Conscious
item.description: Notable thinkers keep telling us they think A.I. is conscious. That doesn’t mean it’s true.

item.title: Trump’s Iran War Is Punishing America’s Working Class
item.description: Higher fuel prices are a burden on low-income families.

item.title: The Midterms Ground Has Shifted
item.description: Two powerful forces in politics clash.

item.title: Mothers and Daughters: Two Stories
item.description: Responses to a guest essay by Molly Jong-Fast, “I Tried to Become My Mother and Ended Up Becoming Myself.” Also: President Trump in China; a gift to Big Tobacco.

item.title: Stop Looking for an ‘Offramp' in Iran
item.description: And no, an exit strategy won’t help you, either.

item.title: Another Trump Appointee Learns It’s Easier to Be a Critic Than a Leader
item.description: We have no F.D.A. director, no permanent C.D.C. director and no surgeon general.

item.title: What Trump’s Magic Math Costs You
item.description: Trump is attacking the data that helps Americans understand reality itself. In this video from Times Opinion, we examine how his war on numbers follows a familiar authoritarian playbook.

item.title: China Is Worried About A.I. Too
item.description: What if you weren’t worried about A.I. taking your job? That seems to be closer to the reality in China, where keeping pace with the new technology is a much bigger focus than economic disruption. On “Interesting Times,” Kyle Chan, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution, explains how the countries differ in their anxieties over artificial intelligence.

item.title: Why China Fears the ‘A.I. Girlfriend’
item.description: China is worried A.I. companions could be a drag on productivity. On “Interesting Times,” Kyle Chan, a foreign policy fellow at the Brookings Institution, tells the Opinion columnist Ross Douthat that the government thinks youth “should be engineering the future and building out the start-ups and the future Chinese versions of SpaceX.”

item.title: The Damage of ‘Trump Math’ Is Adding Up
item.description: Authoritarians go after data. The president has already started.

item.title: Climate Change as a 2026 Campaign Issue
item.description: Readers largely disagree with a guest essay that argued that Democrats should not focus on it. Also: Violence against Latinos; school closings.

item.title: Young Folks Help English Grow Up
item.description: The language is benefiting from a respect for diversity.

item.title: The Trump Dumpster Fire Is Lit
item.description: When MAHA turns into HAHA.

item.title: I Don’t Think You Can Even Call This Hypocrisy
item.description: The secrets of one of the architects of the religious right are being revealed. One of the secrets is that they weren’t really secrets.
